reasoning:
1. Base Rate & Current Environment: The 360-day average of protests (ending May 25, 2025) is approximately 1,025.25 per 30 days. This average was driven extremely high by the eruption of anti-Trump, anti-DOGE, and anti-tariff "days of action" — notably Presidents' Day in February, April 5 (which alone saw over 1,000 protests), April 19, and May Day (May 1). Without these specific massive, synchronized 50-state events, a typical 30-day period relies on the background baseline protest rate, which is likely below ~800-900 per month.
2. June 1 Window (May 2 - May 31): Because May 1 (a massively popular global protest day against the Trump agenda) falls just outside the 30-day window preceding June 1, the ensuing 30 days will have a much harder time hitting 1,025 solely on baseline numbers or the May 25 George Floyd 5th anniversary.
3. Mid-to-Late Year Windows: The windows for late June, August, and November have greater uncertainty. June contains Pride Month and Juneteenth; August has summer seasonality; November includes local off-year elections. If the Trump administration triggers further "days of action", hitting 1,025 is easy simply due to single-day mass mobilization. However, absent pre-planned mega-events currently on the radar, the probability is slightly shaded below 0.5 in the near-to-mid term.
4. Uncertainty scaling: Following instructions, as the forecast horizon extends into late summer and autumn, the chance for new catalysts (which currently can't be foreseen) increases, pushing the probability closer to 0.5.
